disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rash began 1-2 days after an episode of viral gastroenteritis with frequent loose stools
trigger_question_examples: When did the rash start and was he unwell beforehand?
Has he had any diarrhoea or a tummy bug recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started a couple of days after he had a tummy bug — he was doing lots of runny poos and then the rash came up and got worse fast.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rash affects the convex surfaces in contact with the nappy while the deep skin folds appear spared
trigger_question_examples: Where exactly is the rash — is it in the creases or on the rounded parts?
Do the deep skin folds look affected?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's on the round parts that touch the nappy — his bottom, lower tummy and privates. The deep creases in his groin and between his cheeks actually look normal to us.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No small satellite spots spreading beyond the main rash and no candidal features described
trigger_question_examples: Are there any little separate red spots spreading out beyond the edge of the rash?
Any spots in the creases or spreading into the groin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, we haven't seen any little separate spots spreading out — it's one big raw red area on the parts that touch the nappy.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No honey-coloured crusting, pus, blisters or open ulceration on the raw skin
trigger_question_examples: Is there any yellow crusting, pus, or fluid-filled blisters?
Is the skin broken open or oozing yellow crust anywhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No yellow crusts or blisters — the skin is raw and shiny and a bit weepy, but nothing crusty or pus-filled.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reduced feeding for 12 hours because of pain, but still passing urine and mouth appears moist
trigger_question_examples: How has he been feeding and is he still having wet nappies?
Is he drinking, and when was his last wet nappy?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He's been feeding much less for about half a day because it hurts, but he's still having some wet nappies and his mouth looks moist.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Family uses alcohol/fragrance commercial wipes and an ineffective thin water-based lotion
trigger_question_examples: What are you using to clean and protect the area at changes?
What wipes or creams have you been using?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: We clean him with the usual scented baby wipes and we've been dabbing on a thin water-based lotion, but it hasn't helped.
